title: Data, Database Systems, and the Query Journey · Spiral Data Review
summary: A support organization has inconsistent spreadsheets, duplicated customer facts, and no defensible retention process. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a database adoption and data-lifecycle decision record after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace data through creation, processing, review, retention, retrieval, and defensible destruction.
Compare a DBMS with files and spreadsheets using integrity, concurrency, independence, recovery, and query needs.
Trace a query through parser, planner, executor, storage, buffer, transaction, and recovery responsibilities.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Data Lifecycle · changed case · Review Data Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
review case — A review support system has 6 spreadsheets, repeated customer identifiers, conflicting ticket status values, and no destruction date. Trace one data item through its lifecycle and the query-processing components before selecting a storage boundary.

Which action demonstrates Data Lifecycle without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Data Lifecycle · changed case · Review Data Lifecycle.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace data through creation, processing, review, retention, retrieval, and defensible destruction. For Data Lifecycle · changed case · Review Data Lifecycle, keep Data Lifecycle connected to executable relational evidence.

Trace data through creation, processing, review, retention, retrieval, and defensible destruction. Apply it to the stated input and retain a reproducible trace for a database adoption and data-lifecycle decision record.

stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review support system has 2 spreadsheets, repeated customer identifiers, conflicting ticket status values, and no destruction date. Trace one data item through its lifecycle and the query-processing components before selecting a storage boundary.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: If collection is legal, every later use is automatically ethical.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Classify data purpose, owner, sensitivity, quality risk, retention, and downstream harm before storage. Artifact under review: a database adoption and data-lifecycle decision record.

options.text: Run a convenient Data Custodianship query for separate evidence from confidence, omit grain, NULL, cardinality, and dialect checks, then accept one plausible result grid.
The record names the assumption, applies Data Custodianship, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Classify data purpose, owner, sensitivity, quality risk, retention, and downstream harm before storage.
